title: eschscholzia californica subsp. californica
intro: Eschscholzia californica subsp. californica, commonly known as the California poppy, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is also known as the golden poppy, and its bright orange to yellow flowers are a notable feature of the California state flower. The California poppy has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and it is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and it is sometimes used in herbal remedies to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing.
